note: Precision is low because Gold Standard samples 25 entities while graph covers all. Triple Recall is the key comparison metric. Type-III datasets show 0 recall for both systems because hierarchical structures don't decompose into clean (subject, year, value) triples.

wb_maternal.note: 5 unmatched triples (Indonesia 2005-2021) reflect missing data in graph, not evaluation error

inpatient_2023.note: Hierarchical structure - triple extraction not applicable

the_university_ranking.note: Reversed edge fix enabled extraction; Recall=0.560 matches FC=0.567

summary.fix_note: Evaluation script fixed (2026-04-02): Strategy D now handles both edge directions (value_node→subject and subject→value_node) and bare-year node format (mortality_rate_2005=4.6 without 'year' prefix). WB Child Mortality: 0.860→1.000, WB Maternal: 0.493→0.967.
summary.interpretation: Triple F1 Recall confirms FC pattern for Type-II datasets: SGE extracts structured (subject, year, value) triples that Baseline completely fails to produce. Low Precision is expected because Gold Standard is a 25-entity sample of full dataset.
